help.title: Help
help.footer: Tap the back arrow or Close to return.

intro.title: What is this?
intro.body: Sunday Pádel is a tiny tournament tracker for casual groups. Each Telegram group runs its own tournaments. The app remembers who is playing, who paired with whom, every match and the running leaderboard.

registration.title: Registration
registration.body: On the Current tab, toggle ‘Playing’ to add yourself to the next tournament. Toggle ‘BBQ’ if you’ll stay for food. You can change either while registration is open. Once the admin presses ‘Stop registration’, the tournament enters a short review state where the admin locks the roster, confirms teams and assigns courts before starting. Heads-up: if you resign (switch Playing off) you cannot rejoin on your own — ask the admin to unlock you.

teams.title: Pairing up
teams.body: By default the admin forms all teams for you — you’ll just see ‘Admin will form teams’ until they do. If your group allows player pairing, you’ll see the other unpaired players: tap ‘Pair up’ and confirm to form the team. Your previous partner is shown first with a star. You can ‘Leave team’ any time before start — unless the admin has confirmed your team, in which case only resigning will break it.

live.title: During a live tournament
live.body: When the admin starts the tournament, the Submit-a-match form appears. First-round court assignments (green courts 1–2, blue courts 3–5) are shown at the top. Pick the opposing team, type the game score (e.g. 6-4 or 11-8), then tap the Telegram main button (or Submit). One score pair per record — if you play several games, submit one record per game.

matches.title: Confirm and dispute
matches.body: The opposing team should Confirm your submission. If a score is wrong, anyone in the match can Dispute it and the admin can fix or delete the match. Submissions auto-confirm after 30 minutes if no one acts.

leaderboard.title: Leaderboard
leaderboard.body: Teams need a few matches to be ranked (your group’s admin sets the minimum). Ranking uses win rate, then game ratio, then matches played.

history.title: History tab
history.body: Past tournaments show the podium (top 3) for each event so you can see who won what and when.

overall.title: Overall scores
overall.body: Each finished tournament awards podium points (10 / 7 / 5 / 3 / 1) plus a small bonus for every win. The Overall tab is your group’s all-time leaderboard.

admin.title: Admin tools
admin.body: Admins create tournaments and shepherd them through draft → registration → review → live → ended. During registration and review you get a dedicated screen with three sections: Players (add/remove anyone from the group, unlock a resigned player), Teams (form pairs, confirm them to lock the lineup, or disband), and Courts (assign teams to the 5 first-round courts — 1–2 green, 3–5 blue). Starting is blocked until every playing registration is in a confirmed team, the player count is even, and at least one first-round court is assigned. After Start you can still edit/delete results, resolve disputes, and export CSVs of registrations and results. Whether players are allowed to self-pair is a per-group setting that defaults to admin-only.

language.title: Language
language.body: Pick your language from the dropdown at the top — your choice is remembered for this Telegram account across devices.

privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.body: The app only stores what’s needed to run tournaments: your Telegram name, your registrations and the matches you played. Nothing is shared outside your group.

overall.empty: No overall scores yet — end a tournament to start the ranking.
